video:video.video:title: Introducing Money Happens: A new AP audio series
video:video.video:description: Listen to Money Happens, a new audio series where the AP follows how people are dealing with debt, housing and rising costs and provides practical tips from financial experts

video:video.video:title: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?
video:video.video:description: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?

video:video.video:title: Kneecap 'thrive in the chaos' with album 'Fenian' shaped by court case | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The Irish hip-hop group Kneecap recorded their second album "Fenian" while dealing with a legal battle. Liam Óg Ó hAnnaidh, known as Mo Chara, faced terror-related charges for allegedly displaying a Hezbollah flag during a London concert.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish premieres concert film with James Cameron — and confetti
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish and James Cameron pose at the Los Angeles premiere of her "Hit Me Hard and Soft" concert film — then the confetti hits.

video:video.video:title: Venus Williams sparkles on 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Venus Williams sparkles in a black gown on the 2026 carpet.

video:video.video:title: What to know about hantavirus and how it spreads
video:video.video:description: Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread mainly by contact with rodents. It's rare and not easy for a hantavirus to spread between people.

video:video.video:title: Beer, brass and 'Hau-Ruck' shouts as a Bavarian village raises its new maypole
video:video.video:description: It's May, and in the Bavarian village of Kühbach, residents are marking the first day of the month by raising a single decorated tree trunk known as a maypole, a custom observed across southern Germany, Austria and parts of central Europe.

video:video.video:title: With so many aging influencers are we in an era of golden old age?
video:video.video:description: Old age is supposed to be when we step back and relax. But it seems many are stepping forward. If you look online, or in the media, in politics, entertainment or business older people appear to be at their peak.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberations begin in murder retrial of former Ohio deputy who fatally shot Black man
video:video.video:description: A jury in Ohio has begun deliberating in the retrial of former sheriff's deputy Jason Meade, charged with murder and reckless homicide in the fatal shooting of 23-year-old Casey Goodson Jr.

video:video.video:title: Cuba president condemns additional sanctions imposed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has rejected the latest sanctions imposed by the U.S. government against the island — and against anyone who dares to trade with it — and questioned how long the world will continue to tolerate what he considered to be an abusive collective punishment by the U.S. ad

video:video.video:title: A new album and intention for country artist Ashley McBryde
video:video.video:description: Country artist Ashley McBryde suffered a severe injury in 2021, falling from a horse in Montana and landing on her head.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 24-30 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Macron sings at Armenian state dinner with Pashinyan on drums
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron took to the microphone at a state dinner in Yerevan on Monday, with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan accompanying him on the drums.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says he hopes China talks to Iran about opening the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday he hopes Chinese officials talk to Iran about the Strait of Hormuz during a foreign minister's visit this week.

video:video.video:title: Filipino girl group BINI say they're 'blessed' to represent their culture
video:video.video:description: BINI say they're "blessed" to "proudly represent" their culture after making history as the first Filipino girl group to perform at Coachella.
video:video.video:tag: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

video:video.video:title: United Airlines plane strikes a moving truck during landing at Newark
video:video.video:description: A low flying United Airlines plane crashed in a light pole and a moving bakery truck on the New Jersey Turnpike Sunday afternoon after making an attempt to land at Newark Airport.

video:video.video:title: New York City police release footage of machete-wielding man shot at Grand Central
video:video.video:description: New York City police have released body-worn camera footage of officers shooting and killing a machete-wielding man who stabbed three people at a Grand Central subway stop last month.

video:video.video:title: Landlords seek relief from unpaid rent in lawsuit against federal eviction ban
video:video.video:description: A group of landlords are hoping to settle with the federal government over what they say are billions of dollars in losses due to the federal eviction moratorium in place for part of the pandemic.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Mid-term elections in Britain could hasten the end for embattled leader Starmer
video:video.video:description: British voters will cast their ballots on Thursday in elections that could hasten the end of Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s troubled term, and confirm that an increasingly dis-United Kingdom has entered an era of messy multiparty politics.

video:video.video:title: Eilish calls Attenborough 'the GOAT'
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish explains why she admires David Attenborough - who will turn 100 this week - as she promotes her concert movie with iconic director James Cameron.

video:video.video:title: Cruise ship waits for help after suspected outbreak of rare hantavirus onboard kills 3
video:video.video:description: At least three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: A converted church in rural Pennsylvania is becoming an incubator for Amish roots music
video:video.video:description: Ragamuffin Hall in McCoysville was the setting last weekend for a concert by two of the rising stars, Ben and Rose Stoltzfus.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo marks World Press Freedom Day remembering members of media killed at war
video:video.video:description: The United Nations General Assembly proclaimed May 3 as World Press Freedom Day in 1993. 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists worldwide ever recorded, according to The Committee to Protect Journalists.

video:video.video:title: Atlanta Police Department holds mass shooting training ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The Atlanta Police department is preparing its emergency response in case an incident happens during the World Cup. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: Iron Maiden on their mascot, Eddie
video:video.video:description: British heavy metal veterans Iron Maiden love to hate their terrifying mascot, Eddie.

video:video.video:title: States across the wildfire-prone Western U.S. are using AI for early detection
video:video.video:description: AI cameras are playing an increasingly important role in detecting wildfires in the drought-prone U.S. West.

video:video.video:title: Colombia discusses hippo relocation with Vantara after backlash over euthanasia plan
video:video.video:description: The Colombian government on Wednesday announced that Indian conservation initiative Vantara had expressed interest in relocating an invasive population of hippopotamuses in the country.

video:video.video:title: Russia ramps up attempts to kill opponents in Europe, intelligence officials say
video:video.video:description: While Russian officials have long been accused of silencing the country’s enemies abroad, three Western intelligence officials from different countries told AP that a campaign of targeted killings has ramped up since President Vladimir Putin's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 5th: Defense department says ceasefire with Iran still in effect; Romanian government falls as no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan passes; Book publishers sue Meta; Tony Award nominations announced.

video:video.video:title: Hospitals in southern Lebanon face uphill struggle as they treat the wounded and displaced
video:video.video:description: Hospitals in southern Lebanon are under immense strain and overwhelmed due to the dual pressures of war injuries and displacement. (AP Video: Bassam Hatoum

video:video.video:title: Convoy of oil trucks shows war-battered Syria selling itself as safe corridor amid regional conflict
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of oil-laden trucks, some arriving from Iraq, lined up along Syria's coastal road on Friday as they wait to unload into tankers at Baniyas port.

video:video.video:title: Police search for suspects in Oklahoma shooting that sent at least 18 people to hospitals
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, some critically, according to police and hospital officials.

video:video.video:title: A$AP Rocky on the Met Gala carpet: 'We feel how we look'
video:video.video:description: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky made a joint arrival at the Met Gala, closing out the carpet in signature fashion. Reflecting on their look, Rocky said, “We feel how we look.”

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame

video:video.video:title: WHO says hantavirus risk remains low globally, confirms eight cases and three deaths
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization (WHO) said Wednesday samples taken from a cruise ship that is in the midst of an outbreak of hantavirus had tested positive for the Andes strain of the virus.

video:video.video:title: Gigi Hadid shares what the Met Gala means to her
video:video.video:description: Model Gigi Hadid tells AP about the Meta Gala and fashion education.

video:video.video:title: Long DACA renewal wait times leave some 'Dreamers' without status, a job and fearing detainment
video:video.video:description: Renewal wait times for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that allows people who were brought to the U.S. as children to temporarily remain in the country and work have increased dramatically in the past year.

video:video.video:title: Families evacuated from Gaza enjoy a day to decompress at Rome's ancient baths
video:video.video:description: The Baths of Caracalla, the sumptuously decorated public baths complex near the Colosseum, were long a place of leisure, health care and relaxation for ancient Romans.

video:video.video:title: At least one dead in Russian strike on kindergarten in Ukraine's Sumy
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and two injured in a Russian strike on a kindergarten in in Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Wednesday, Ukrainian State Emergency Service said.

video:video.video:title: Yuji the baby monkey finds comfort in plush teddy
video:video.video:description: Yuji, the baby patas monkey, is winning hearts and fans for his love of a stuffed teddy bear. Little Yuji was rejected by his mother at birth who was not able to care for him, so now he is receiving the love and care from the expert staff at Guadalajara Zoo in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Justice Department seeks whistleblowers to combat antitrust in beef industry
video:video.video:description: Top officials in the U.S. Justice Department and Department of Agriculture said the administration is seeking whistleblowers to combat anti-competitive practices in the beef industry.

video:video.video:title: Ford is back with employee pricing for all as gas and car prices continue to climb
video:video.video:description: Ford Motor Co. is offering employee pricing to customers, as gas prices in the U.S. hit new multiyear highs and the cost of a new vehicle hovers near $50,000 (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: A solar-powered charging station in central Cuba brings life to a darkened island
video:video.video:description: A free, solar-powered charging station, or “solinera” as it’s known, has opened in Santa Clara, in Cuba’s central region.

video:video.video:title: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs is found not guilty of assaulting his private chef
video:video.video:description: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs has been found not guilty of assaulting his private chef in a pay dispute.

video:video.video:title: Planned Parenthood: Goal of mail-order abortion pill ban is akin to a 'national abortion ban'
video:video.video:description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug in what was the biggest jolt to abortion policy since the overturning of Roe v.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Health official in Ushuaia doubts Tierra del Fuego is source of suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A health official in Ushuaia expressed doubts that Tierra del Fuego was the source of a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with nearly 150 people on aboard, after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill.

video:video.video:title: Children in Gaza find relief from war trauma through breakdancing
video:video.video:description: Breakdance is helping Palestinian children in Gaza cope with the trauma of war. In the Nuseirat refugee camp, boys and girls from different ages gather every week to dance among the rubble, finding moments of relief and support by dancing.

video:video.video:title: Denver families dust off winter gear for spring snowstorm
video:video.video:description: A late spring snowstorm eased across parts of Colorado after closing schools, delaying flights and turning roads slushy on Wednesday. (AP Video by Thomas Peipert)

video:video.video:title: Rio favela brings back street art tradition for World Cup
video:video.video:description: World Cup celebrations are already underway in Rocinha, Rio de Janeiro’s largest favela, on Tuesday, as local artists and residents painted an important commercial street.

video:video.video:title: New US postage stamps capture essence of historic Route 66
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Postal Service is releasing a new series of stamps to honor Route 66’s centennial. The offering includes eight stamps — one each for the states traversed by the Mother Road.

video:video.video:title: Banksy confirms central London statue of man blinded by flag is his
video:video.video:description: A new sculpture in central London of a man striding off a plinth with his face blinded by a billowing flag was claimed Thursday by the elusive street artist Banksy, more than a day after it mysteriously appeared.

video:video.video:title: Naomi Osaka leaves hotel for Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Naomi Osaka stunned as she left The Mark Hotel for the Met Gala in a dramatic Robert Wun white sculptural fitted dress with exaggerated shoulders and adorned with red feathers and a matching headpiece.

video:video.video:title: A citizen campaign returns iconic kiwi birds to New Zealand's capital after century-long absence
video:video.video:description: The kiwi, New Zealand’s sacred national bird, vanished from the hills around Wellington more than a century ago, but now the capital's residents are waging an improbable citizen campaign to return the endangered flightless birds to the city.

video:video.video:title: Zoo in New Orleans welcomes rare Hartmann’s mountain zebras for the first time
video:video.video:description: Hartmann’s mountain zebra are listed as a species vulnerable to extinction due to habitat loss from livestock farming, climate-induced drought, and competition for water.

video:video.video:title: Mick Jagger breaks down the meaning of 'Rough and Twisted'
video:video.video:description: As The Rolling Stones announce the July 10 release of new album “Foreign Tongues,” Mick Jagger explains "Rough and Twisted," first released under an alias in April.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse video shows French aircraft carrier strike group transiting the Suez Canal
video:video.video:description: The nuclear-powered Charles de Gaulle is moving towards the Red Sea in preparation for a potential French-British mission in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Western states are increasingly using AI to spot wildfires
video:video.video:description: With a severe wildfire season forecast for the Western U.S., some communities are trying to get ahead of it using AI-monitored cameras.

video:video.video:title: Spain's Sanchez accuses Israel and Netanyahu of 'illegal abduction' of Gaza flotilla activists
video:video.video:description: Israeli authorities said they were taking two high-profile activists who led an aid flotilla bound for Gaza, and who were captured by Israel in international waters of the Mediterranean Sea, to Israel for questioning.

video:video.video:title: Spirit Airlines goes out of business after 34 years, ending operations immediately
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines, an impish upstart that shook the industry with its irreverent ads and deep discount fares, announced Saturday that it has gone out of business after 34 years.

video:video.video:title: Assault trial of former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs begins with contrasting accounts
video:video.video:description: Former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs’ former private chef testifies in his assault trial.

video:video.video:title: Leaders gather in Yerevan for European Political Community meeting as Ukraine and Iran wars loom
video:video.video:description: The summit will be co-chaired by European Council President António Costa and Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan.

video:video.video:title: Events mark Missing and Murdered Indigenous Peoples awareness day
video:video.video:description: Across the country, Indigenous people are gathering this week to honor loved ones who are missing or have been killed and to call for better data collection, law enforcement response and reforms to make their communities safer.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus outbreak: 3 patients evacuated from cruise ship, new case confirmed in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: South African health authorities have identified the Andes strain of hantavirus in two passengers who were on a cruise ship at the center of an outbreak of the rare infection.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth, opponents disagree
video:video.video:description: A supporter of the ban points to FDA "safeguards" in place when the drug was approved. (AP video: Joseph B. Frederick)

video:video.video:title: Fashion expert weighs in on 2026 Met Gala looks and theme
video:video.video:description: Fashion writer Leigh Nordstrom of Women's Wear Daily highlights the most memorable looks and trends at the 2026 Met Gala including Beyoncé, Kim Kardashian and Eileen Gu.

video:video.video:title: Hazen Fire burns more than 900 acres in rural Arizona
video:video.video:description: Firefighters battle the Hazen Fire burning Near Buckeye, Arizona; the fire sparked on Saturday afternoon is estimated at 980 acres

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court weakens the Voting Rights Act, hollowing out law that protects minority voting rights
video:video.video:description: Six decades ago, the Voting Rights Act became one of the most consequential laws in the nation’s history. On Wednesday, the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge orders Louisiana to allow New Orleans exoneree to assume elected clerk office
video:video.video:description: A Louisiana exoneree who won election to a New Orleans clerk seat began work Monday after a federal judge temporarily blocked the state from eliminating the position.

video:video.video:title: May Day protesters block roadway at SFO's international terminal
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a road outside the international terminal at San Francisco’s airport on Friday, leading to the street's closure for about two hours.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts UFC fighters ahead of White House event in June
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump summoned reporters to the Oval Office on Wednesday touting the upcoming Ultimate Fighting Championship match that will take place at the White House in June.

video:video.video:title: Learn the Eurovision dance moves
video:video.video:description: Eurovision 2026 contestants contestants teach the dance routines for their songs.

video:video.video:title: Virologist on the hantavirus outbreak on board a cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Giulia Gallo, Postdoctoral Scientist in the Viral Glycoproteins Group at The Pirbright Institute told The Associated Press that "the most important thing is not to create panic" as the transmission rate for the virus is very low.

video:video.video:title: Amputee footballers in Rwanda find healing and a sense of community
video:video.video:description: Amputee football offers a sense of community and healing in Rwanda, a country that went through its darkest time in 1994 during its genocide.

video:video.video:title: Body camera video shows moment house explodes as NYPD responds to call
video:video.video:description: A massive, fiery explosion at a house in Queens sent several New York City police officers flying through the air early Thursday morning as authorities were responding to a call of a man inside armed with a knife and the smell of gas.

video:video.video:title: May Day rally in New York targets Amazon
video:video.video:description: May Day demonstrators in New York speak out against Amazon, which they say has been pushing small business out of communities.

video:video.video:title: Traditional welcome for Taiwan president as he visits Eswatini king
video:video.video:description: Taiwan president Lai Ching-te is welcomed by Eswatini King Mswati III with a military ceremony at Lozitha Palace where they take part in a signing ceremony, bolstering ties with one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies despite growing international pressure from China.

video:video.video:title: Drone footage shows cruise ship at the center of a rare hantavirus outbreak waiting off Cape Verde
video:video.video:description: Three passengers have died and at least four people have been sick on board the MV Hondius, a Dutch cruise ship, in what health officials say is an outbreak of hantavirus, which usually spreads by inhaling contaminated rodent droppings.

video:video.video:title: Views from inside the cruise ship after suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean on Monday after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus, according to the World Health Organization

video:video.video:title: Trump says he is reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he was reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Current and former Mississippi lawmakers prepare for a new chapter in the fight for voting rights
video:video.video:description: Former Mississippi State Rep. Edward Blackmon Jr. and his son, State Sen. Bradford Blackmon, reflect on their generational fight for voting rights after a recent U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Police in Istanbul detain hundreds trying to get to Taksim Square in May Day Demo
video:video.video:description: Police detained at least 15 protesters who were trying to reach Istanbul's Taksim Square Friday, the epicenter of landmark 2013 protests, in defiance of a government ban.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs arrives in court for assault trial
video:video.video:description: Jury selection begins in the assault trial of former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs. Diggs pleaded not guilty in February to charges stemming from an alleged dispute with his former private chef.

video:video.video:title: Inside the Rolling Stones’ album event
video:video.video:description: Mick Jagger, Ronnie Wood and Keith Richards talk about upcoming Rolling Stones album, "Foreign Tongues," before a New Yok audience including Leonardo DiCaprio.

video:video.video:title: Is the UK's traditional fish and chip supper under threat?
video:video.video:description: Britain’s traditional fish and chip supper is under pressure, from rising fish prices and tighter catch limits to the knock on effects of global conflict and higher energy costs.

video:video.video:title: Former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs arrives in court
video:video.video:description: A woman who worked as a live-in chef for Stefon Diggs and is accusing the former New England Patriots wide receiver of assault is expected to continue testimony at his trial, which is marked by sharply conflicting accounts of what happened inside his home.

video:video.video:title: Charli xcx poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Charli xcx poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores telehealth access to abortion pill mifepristone
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court on Monday restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: CNN founder Ted Turner, a pioneer of cable TV news, dies at 87
video:video.video:description: Ted Turner, a brash television pioneer who raced yachts, owned huge chunks of the American West and transformed the news business by launching CNN and introducing the 24-hour cable news cycle, died Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Man accused of attacking OpenAI CEO's home pleads not guilty to attempted murder
video:video.video:description: The man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the San Francisco home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman pleaded not guilty to charges of attempted murder and attempted arson.

video:video.video:title: Riders put hobby horses through their paces at Germany's second championship
video:video.video:description: It was giddy up and go in Frankfurt on Saturday as the country's hobby horsers took part in their second ever championships.

video:video.video:title: Pen pal programs and letter writing are back in fashion among young people
video:video.video:description: Letter writing, like vinyls and analogue photography, is making a come back. In 2021, the U.S. Postal Service sent cards and envelopes to 25,000 elementary school classrooms for a pen pal project, but older students also are putting pen to paper

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 5
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, May 5th: U.S. says two U.S.-flagged merchant ships went through strait of Hormuz; Iranian attack on U.A.E. reported; Hantavirus outbreak suspected on cruise ship in Atlantic; Police seek suspects in Oklahoma shooting.

video:video.video:title: A driver plows into people in a shopping area in Germany, killing 2
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a driver has plowed into people in a busy shopping area in the center of the German city of Leipzig, leaving two people dead.

video:video.video:title: Amputee football players in Rwanda find healing and sense of community
video:video.video:description: Amputee football offers a sense of community and healing in Rwanda, a country that went through its darkest time in 1994 during its genocide.

video:video.video:title: US and Iran claim attacks on each other in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says it fired on Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran claimed to have struck a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo calls out Trump’s misrepresentation of his views on Iran and nuclear weapons
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo responded to Trump's latest criticism by calling out the U.S. president's misrepresentation of his views.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill U.S.

video:video.video:title: Thousands evacuated overnight as forest fires cuts through Tuscany landscape
video:video.video:description: By midday on Friday, firefighters had five Canadair planes and three water-dropping helicopters working to put out the blaze.

video:video.video:title: Britney Spears avoids jail time in California DUI case
video:video.video:description: Britney Spears has pleaded guilty to a lesser charge that will allow her to avoid jail time after California prosecutors accused her of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs.

video:video.video:title: Stabbing at Washington state high school wounds 6, including suspect, police say
video:video.video:description: A student at a Tacoma, Washington high school was booked on five counts of first-degree assault after four students and an adult security guard were wounded in a stabbing at the school Thursday, police said.

video:video.video:title: Kendall Jenner poses at 2026 Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Kendall Jenner poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet.

video:video.video:title: Zac Posen dressed Kendall Jenner as a Greek goddess at the Met Gala. Here's how
video:video.video:description: Zac Posen had long wanted to dress Kendall Jenner, and now he has - on one of fashion’s biggest stages, the Met Gala red carpet, where he dressed her as a Greek goddess.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III's charity celebrates 50 years of helping young people find work with a gala in NYC
video:video.video:description: As King Charles III and Queen Camilla continued their first U.S. state visit since he became king, they highlighted The King’s Trust at a New York gala Wednesday in New York, as the charity expands its impact in more than two dozen countries.

video:video.video:title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City injures at least 10
video:video.video:description: Police in an Oklahoma City suburb say that at least 10 people have been taken to hospitals following a shooting at a party at a lake.

video:video.video:title: Korean community member says area where Carrollton shooting happened is typically peaceful
video:video.video:description: Police say a man shot five people in Texas on Tuesday, killing two of them, at a shopping center north of Dallas in an area known as Koreatown in Carrollton.

video:video.video:title: Venice Biennale previews in chaos as war follows art into world's oldest exhibition
video:video.video:description: The Venice Biennale has begun previewing its 61st edition, just days after the contemporary art show's jury resigned over the participation of Israel and Russia.

video:video.video:title: At least 5 killed and 12 injured after Russian air strike on Kramatorsk
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and 12 others injured after a Russian airstrike on Kramatorsk on Tuesday, local authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Democrats accuse Commerce Secretary Lutnick of evading questions on ties to Epstein
video:video.video:description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick appeared Wednesday before a House committee investigating sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, trying to explain to lawmakers his contact with the financier after Epstein's 2008 conviction for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl.

video:video.video:title: France reckons with Nazi-looted art in new Paris museum gallery
video:video.video:description: Last month, the museum launched its first research unit dedicated to tracing the orphans’ rightful heirs, file by file.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, May 6th: Trump pauses effort to guide ships through Strait of Hormuz; U.S. gas prices up 50% since the Iran war began; Vivek Ramaswamy, Dr.

video:video.video:title: Timmy the humpback whale released in the North Sea after being stranded off Germany for weeks
video:video.video:description: Nicknamed Timmy by German media, the whale was spotted swimming near Germany’s Baltic Sea coast on March 3, far from its natural habitat in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Thousands rally for workers rights outside Phildelphia's City Hall
video:video.video:description: Thousands rally for workers rights outside Phildelphia's City Hall

video:video.video:title: Opening statements begin in assault trial of former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs chef dispute
video:video.video:description: Opening statements have begun in the assault trial of Stefon Diggs, a former New England Patriots wide receiver accused of assaulting his private chef.

video:video.video:title: Memphians head to state capitol to protest redistricting efforts
video:video.video:description: Galvanized by redistricting efforts that threaten to eliminate Tennessee's only majority-Black Democratic congressional district, Memphians waited in cold, rainy darkness Wednesday morning to board a bus that would take them to protest at the state capitol.

video:video.video:title: EU sustainability rules push luxury fashion house Ferragamo to map leather origins
video:video.video:description: Luxury fashion brand Ferragamo is the first to provide figures on material traceability, particularly for leather.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Beirut suburbs for first time since ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs Wednesday for the first time since a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group was announced on April 17.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti poses at 2026 Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti poses in a colorful gown at her first Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 3: UK reports cargo ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz; Ukraine hits Russian oil port and tankers; Migrant boat runs aground in France, 2 dead; Shakira draws 2M to free Rio beach concert.

video:video.video:title: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy wins Ohio’s GOP nomination for governor
video:video.video:description: Billionaire biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy has clinched the Republican nomination for Ohio governor and will face off this fall against the state’s COVID-era health director, Democrat Amy Acton.

video:video.video:title: Amtrak may ease gun restrictions on trains despite security concerns
video:video.video:description: Amtrak is considering allowing passengers to store guns in lockboxes on most trains, raising security concerns after an alleged attempt on President Donald Trump’s life involving a rail traveler.

video:video.video:title: Daily life for displaced in Gaza Strip still defined by scarcity, exposure and exhaustion
video:video.video:description: Across makeshift camps, daily life is defined by scarcity, exposure and exhaustion. Tents are torn and unfit for any season, offering little protection from winter rain or the harsh summer heat.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy proposes drone defence partnership with Bahrain’s King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met Tuesday with Bahrain’s King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, proposing a drone defence partnership and discussing shared security threats including Iranian strikes and tensions around the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Democrat Amy Acton says it’s time to make Ohio affordable again after winning Governor nomination
video:video.video:description: Amy Acton, who won the uncontested Democratic primary in the Ohio governor’s race, said during a victory speech that she was running to make the state more affordable again.

video:video.video:title: Mexicans recreate Cinco de Mayo victory over French army in the Battle of Puebla
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people dressed in costumes representing soldiers marked Tuesday the anniversary of the 1862 victory by Mexican troops over invading French forces at the Battle of Puebla.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a United plane crashes into a truck injuring the driver
video:video.video:description: A low flying United Airlines plane crashed in a light pole and a moving bakery truck on the New Jersey Turnpike Sunday afternoon after making an attempt to land at Newark Airport.

video:video.video:title: Nicole Kidman poses on the 2026 Met Gala Carpet
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Nicole Kidman poses on the 2026 carpet with her daughter Sunday Rose.

video:video.video:title: Collaborator celebrates Attenboroughs 'extraordinary legacy'
video:video.video:description: Alastair Fothergill, director of Silverback Films and producer of many of Sir David Attenborough's documentaries, discusses the British naturalist's legacy and personal standards ahead of celebrations for Attenborough's 100th birthday.

video:video.video:title: It's a good start but uncertainty looms as Croatia braces for summer tourism season
video:video.video:description: With summer a few months away the streets of Croatia's old town of Dubrovnik are already teeming with tourists.

video:video.video:title: Inside the Rolling Stones’ exclusive album event
video:video.video:description: Mick Jagger, Ronnie Wood and Keith Richards talk about upcoming Rolling Stones album, "Foreign Tongues," before a New York audience including Leonardo DiCaprio. (May 6)

video:video.video:title: Macron sings Aznavour classic at state dinner with Armenian PM Pashinyan on drums
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron took to the microphone at a state dinner in Yerevan on Monday, with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan accompanying him on the drums.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision contestants teach dance routines for their songs
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest hopefuls - including Austria's Cosmó, Germany's Sarah Engels, Ukraine's Leléka, Estonia's Vanilla Ninja, and Senhit with Boy George for San Marino - demonstrate their dance moves ahead of the annual event.

video:video.video:title: Three people injured in a Ukrainian drone attack in Russia's Cheboksary
video:video.video:description: Unconfirmed reports of local media said that a defence enterprise, local college, shopping centre and residential buildings were damaged.

video:video.video:title: Russian drone attack in Ukraine's Ternopil city wounds 12, local officials say
video:video.video:description: The Ukrainian city of Ternopil was targeted by at least 36 drones on Friday wounding at least 12, the regional military administration said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Annual pilgrimage to the El Ghriba Synagogue on the island of Djerba
video:video.video:description: The annual Jewish pilgrimage to the El Ghriba Synagogue drew a modest but notable return of foreign visitors this year, as hundreds gathered under tight security nearly three years after a deadly attack disrupted the centuries-old tradition.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says Iran claims of not wanting a nuke are not backed up by actions
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday that Iran must make a choice between war and peace but that peace will require a convincing demonstration that the Iranian government won’t attempt to pursue atomic arms.

video:video.video:title: Experts warn of rising lead risks in Africa’s solar energy boom
video:video.video:description: Years after a toxic battery recycling plant shut down in Kenya’s coastal city of Mombasa, residents of Owino Uhuru village said they are still living with the scars of lead poisoning, a warning, experts say, of a growing public health risk as Africa’s clean energy boom drives a surge in battery use.

video:video.video:title: Alternative soccer team in Poland resists 'aggressive nationalist stadium culture'
video:video.video:description: An “alternative” soccer team formed by fans to resist what they called "aggressive nationalist stadium culture" is finding new relevance in Poland, as the country is led by a president who does not deny his own past participation in fights among fans.

video:video.video:title: Man describes getting shot at Oklahoma party
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, some critically, according to police and hospital officials.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 1st: Prosecutors release surveillance video from first moment of Correspondents Dinner shooting; Louisiana Congressional primaries suspended; Congress faces War Powers Act deadline over Iran war; Stabbing attack in high school near Seattle.

video:video.video:title: AP Dispatch: European leaders react to U.S. troop withdrawal from Germany
video:video.video:description: European leaders see U.S. President Donald Trump's troop drawdown from Germany as new proof they must go it alone. (AP video shot by Mark Carlson)

video:video.video:title: Stanley Tucci and Emily Blunt receive stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Meryl Streep and Matt Damon were among the stars singing Tucci and Blunt's praises at the ceremony. The two "Devil Wears Prada" actors are also in-laws since Tucci married Blunt's sister, Felicity in 2012.

video:video.video:title: The founding fathers didn’t create a Christian nation but religion did shape their thinking
video:video.video:description: The long-running debate over the founders’ intentions about religion has been turbocharged with the nation's approaching 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Fire at New York City's theater halts 'Book of Mormon'
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Monday in the Broadway theater that hosts “The Book of Mormon,” forcing the long-running musical to close, at least temporarily, as the historic venue undergoes repairs.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of first Israeli strike on Beirut suburbs since the ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs Wednesday for the first time since a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group was announced on April 17.

video:video.video:title: Blue Ivy poses on 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Blue Ivy walks the Met Gala carpet with her parents Beyoncé and Jay-Z.

video:video.video:title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, his spokesman says
video:video.video:description: Rudolph Giuliani, the ex-mayor of New York City and a former adviser to President Donald Trump, is in critical but stable condition at a hospital, his spokesman said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: UAE, Bahrain condemn Iranian strikes, urge Security Council to act
video:video.video:description: The United Arab Emirates and Bahrain are condemning the recent attacks on the UAE, including critical civilian infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: Katy Perry walks the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Katy Perry arrives at the 2026 Met Gala in a custom white Stella McCartney gown.

video:video.video:title: How a court case and loyal fans shaped Kneecap’s second record “FENIAN”
video:video.video:description: Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap were inspired by a legal battle while recording their second record “FENIAN,” even sampling the chants of support from their fans waiting outside of the court.

video:video.video:title: Wild animals are making their homes next to ours. Experts say it’s only natural
video:video.video:description: Spring is when wildlife nest or bear young, often close to humans. Experts say homeowners should enjoy their new neighbors but leave nests and young animals alone.

video:video.video:title: Fire at a shopping center in Iran kills 8 people and injures 40
video:video.video:description: Tehran's emergency service department says a fire in a shopping center near Tehran has killed eight people and injured 40.

video:video.video:title: Passenger says atmosphere on board ship at centre of hantavirus outbreak is 'relatively good'
video:video.video:description: Helene Goessaert, a passenger of a cruise ship at the center of a rare-virus outbreak, says that the atmosphere on board the ship is 'relatively good'.

video:video.video:title: Isabel Allende gives 'The House of the Spirits' TV adaptation her stamp of approval
video:video.video:description: Best-selling author Isabel Allende has given Prime Video's "The House of the Spirits" her stamp of approval.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus outbreak is not the next COVID pandemic, says WHO epidemiologist
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak is not the next COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Parents mourn their son as a town in Argentina faces its own hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Daisy Morinigo and David Delgado said they initially thought their 14-year-old son had the flu when he came down with a fever and body aches.

video:video.video:title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship raises questions about source and spread
video:video.video:description: A rodent-borne illness is suspected of causing an outbreak aboard a cruise ship that has killed three people and sickened others.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Cubans struggle to survive on pocket-sized government ration book as products dwindle
video:video.video:description: Cuba's pocket-sized government ration book has been circulating for more than six decades. It used to offer a bounty of products, but that offer has dwindled in recent years as the island's economic crisis deepened.

video:video.video:title: Connor Storrie arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Connor Storrie at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Shakira's free concert on Copacabana beach draws 2 million fans
video:video.video:description: The performance followed similar shows by Madonna in 2024 and Lady Gaga last year — also attended by huge crowds that danced on the sprawling sands.

video:video.video:title: Most active volcano in the Philippines spews giant ash column sky high
video:video.video:description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said the alert level remained at 3, based on its latest 24-hour monitoring.

video:video.video:title: Three hantavirus patients evacuated from cruise ship off Cape Verde
video:video.video:description: Three patients were evacuated from a cruise ship off Cape Verde on Wednesday amid a hantavirus outbreak, as a new case was confirmed in Switzerland.

video:video.video:title: Your climate impact doesn't end when you die. More people are considering 'greener' death options
video:video.video:description: As interest in alternative forms of burial has risen in recent years, some have turned to green, or conservation burials, to reduce the environmental impact of their passing, such as at the Prairie Creek Conservation Cemetery outside Gainesville, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Walk in the shoes of a Met Gala attendee
video:video.video:description: A look at the route guests at the Met Gala take before posing for photographers and walking up the famous steps to enter the Metropolitan Museum of Art. (May 4)

video:video.video:title: Chinese and Iranian foreign ministers discuss need for a ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, in Beijing on Wednesday morning.

video:video.video:title: Workers around the world demonstrate on May Day
video:video.video:description: People across the world kicked off May Day with large marches and protests as many workers grapple with rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power tied to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Protesters thrown out of legislative hearings on redistricting congressional map targeting Memphis
video:video.video:description: In Tennessee, protesters interrupted legislative hearings on a map that would reshape the majority-Black congressional district in Memphis, prompting state troopers to clear the room.

video:video.video:title: Indiana Black voters, candidates raise concerns about potential new redistricting efforts
video:video.video:description: An effort pushed by U.S. President Donald Trump last November to redraw Indiana’s congressional map failed.

video:video.video:title: Bear cub in Russian animal shelter makes friends with Caucasian Shepherd puppy
video:video.video:description: A bear cub living in a Moscow region animal shelter, after it was found alone in the wild in Russia, has made friends with a Caucasian Shepherd puppy.

video:video.video:title: Spring plans meet snow in Denver as a late storm could be the season's biggest
video:video.video:description: A late snowstorm swept over the Rocky Mountains and into the High Plains on Tuesday, bringing heavy, wet accumulation north of Denver into southeastern Wyoming.

video:video.video:title: Alex Zanardi, Italian auto racing champion who turned Paralympic gold medalist, dies at 59
video:video.video:description: Paying tribute to Zanardi, Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni said on X that Italy had lost "a great champion and an extraordinary man".

video:video.video:title: Chilean puppet show '31 Minutos' draws massive crowd in Mexico City
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans gathered Thursday evening in Mexico City's central square to watch a free live performance of the Chilean television show "31 Minutos" to mark Children’s Day.

video:video.video:title: Vienna theater offers daily scaffolding tours of Klimt ceiling paintings
video:video.video:description: For more than a century, only conservators and experts could catch a close-up glimpse of early works by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt in Vienna.

video:video.video:title: Experts urge ethical debate as cosmetic procedures become the norm
video:video.video:description: As cosmetic interventions become more pervasive, people are grappling with the philosophical and ethical implications of turning to them.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration claims its war in Iran ended with April ceasefire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is arguing that the war in Iran has already ended because of the ceasefire that began in April, an interpretation that would allow the White House to avoid a federal law requirement for congressional approval of military action that extends beyond 60 days.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures attack on Nun near Jerusalem’s old city
video:video.video:description: Israeli police said Friday that they arrested a 36-year-old caught on video attacking a nun in the latest incident targeting Christians near Jerusalem's Old City.

video:video.video:title: Taiwanese grandmothers aged 89 and 91 train at the gym to stay healthy
video:video.video:description: Taiwan has become a super-aged society with a fifth of its people over the age of 65, but seniors in Taipei City are proving that getting older is not a barrier to health.

video:video.video:title: Shakira fans gather in Rio ahead of open-air performance on Copacabana Beach
video:video.video:description: The singer is performing a massive open-air concert along the waterfront in the city's iconic Copacabana Beach next Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will reduce number of troops in Germany 'a lot further' than withdrawal of 5,000
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday the U.S. will reduce number of troops in Germany 'a lot further' than withdrawal of 5,000, escalating a dispute with Chancellor Friedrich Merz as he seeks to scale back America’s commitment to European security.

video:video.video:title: Workers around the world demonstrate on May Day
video:video.video:description: People across the world kicked off May Day with large marches and protests as many workers grapple with rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power tied to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Milly Alcock’s 'punk rock' Supergirl takes flight
video:video.video:description: Milly Alcock says she sees a lot of herself in Supergirl, and jokes about how surreal it is to have her own action figure. (May 2)

video:video.video:title: Habitat for Humanity is developing a new Atlanta community with help from the Carters' initiative
video:video.video:description: In only five days, nearly 1,000 volunteers with Habitat for Humanity will complete 24 affordable housing units in Atlanta's Sylvan Hills neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma mayor responds after shooting injures at least 23 partygoers
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, officials said.

video:video.video:title: US says 'Project Freedom' will defend shipping in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces launched an effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz, with the first two ships, both American-flagged merchant vessels, sailing through unscathed.

video:video.video:title: Protesters march at Tennessee's capitol as lawmakers look to carve up a majority-Black district in Memphis
video:video.video:description: Protesters marched and shouted "Shame!" at Tennessee Republicans who called a special legislative session that could potentially carve up a majority-Black district in Memphis.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Actress Anne Hathaway makes her debut at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt with police as people in Manila march to the US Embassy on May Day
video:video.video:description: Workers in the Philippines clashed with police near the US Embassy in the capital Manila on Friday during their Labor Day protest. (AP video by Joeal Calupitan)

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberating in assault trial of former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs
video:video.video:description: The defense and prosecution deliver closing arguments in the assault trial of former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs after calling multiple witnesses to the stand

video:video.video:title: Trump calls Iran conflict a 'skirmish,' insists US has 'total control'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump downplayed tensions with Iran Tuesday, calling the conflict a “skirmish” and claiming the U.S. has “total control.”

video:video.video:title: Rare Franklin collection on display in Philadelphia before auction
video:video.video:description: The Library Company of Philadelphia — one of several institutions Franklin himself founded — is hosting selections from the Jay T.

video:video.video:title: Severe weather leaves shipwrecks strewn across the Queensland coast
video:video.video:description: Dozens of vessels lie wrecked along the coastline of northern Australia after a season of severe summer weather, leaving state authorities to handle the cleanup.

video:video.video:title: Romania’s pro-European coalition collapses after no-confidence vote against prime minister
video:video.video:description: The joint effort was launched April 28 when the leftist Social Democratic Party, or PSD, which withdrew from the coalition in late April, and the hard-right opposition Alliance for the Unity of Romanians party, or AUR, submitted the motion to Parliament.

video:video.video:title: Is the UK's traditional fish and chip supper under threat?
video:video.video:description: For over a decade Brockley’s Rock in south east London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront, after opening in 2011, building on experience in the trade that dates back to 1989.

video:video.video:title: Surging gas prices, 52 percent higher than before th Iran war, raise costs of summer road trips
video:video.video:description: Rising gas prices are straining drivers' wallets, which could impact summer travel plans if the war in Iran drags on. (AP Video by Terry Chea)

video:video.video:title: USA should not be at Venice Biennale, says artist Anish Kapoor
video:video.video:description: A leading British artist has said the US should have been excluded from the Venice Biennale. Anish Kapoor made the comments amid controversy over the participation of Russia and Israel too.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 2 in the Caribbean
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says it’s launched another strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea. The attack on Monday killed two people.

video:video.video:title: Demos held in Latin American countries to mark Labor Day
video:video.video:description: Demonstrators turned out in Venezuela, Cuba, Ecuador and Argentina. In Mexico City, sex workers held a protest demanding dignity and protections.

video:video.video:title: Leaders arrive at European Political Community summit in Armenia
video:video.video:description: European Union leaders and regional partners have arrived in Armenia to discuss regional and international issues at the 8th European Political Community summit.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched another strike Tuesday on a vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three men.

video:video.video:title: A rich pour of heritage: Yemeni coffeehouses rise across the US
video:video.video:description: Yemeni coffeehouses are rapidly expanding across the U.S., blending centuries-old coffee traditions with modern café culture and serving as community-focused social spaces.

video:video.video:title: South African police hoist crocodile from crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
video:video.video:description: A South African police officer was lowered alone into a "crocodile-infested river" in a daring effort to recover human remains.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish: David Attenborough is 'the GOAT'
video:video.video:description: Musician and climate campaigner Billie Eilish explains why she admires David Attenborough so much, as she promotes new concert movie "Billie Eilish – Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour" with iconic director James Cameron.

video:video.video:title: Fire at a shopping center in Iran kills several people and injures dozens
video:video.video:description: A fire in a shopping center near Iran’s capital killed several people and injured dozens, state TV said Wednesday. The fire started Tuesday

video:video.video:title: Trump jokes that US Navy will take on Cuba on the way home from Iran
video:video.video:description: “Cuba’s got problems,” Trump said in his speech before the non-profit Forum Club of the Palm Beaches at the Raymond F. Kravis Center for the Performing Arts.

video:video.video:title: Spanish Royal House releases video of Princess Leonor on an instruction flight
video:video.video:description: The Spanish Royal House released video on Tuesday of Princess Leonor de Borbon y Ortiz undertaking an instruction flight.

video:video.video:title: Protest groups block access to Russian pavilion at Venice Biennale
video:video.video:description: Russia’s participation in the first Biennale since the 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine led to Russian punk group Pussy Riot and members of Ukrainian feminist organization FEMEN rushing the exhibition area.

video:video.video:title: 2 killed after Russian drone attack in Ukraine's southern Odesa region
video:video.video:description: The drones also hit port infrastructure, causing a fire that was later extinguished by emergency teams, the emergency service reported.

video:video.video:title: Beyoncé returns to Met Gala carpet after 10 years
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Beyoncé poses on the carpet for the first time in 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices sank, and stock markets burst higher worldwide
video:video.video:description: The price for a barrel of Brent fell below $102 Wednesday. On Wall Street, the S&P 500 climbed 1.5% and reached another record.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants to make a deal but he's 'not satisfied with it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he’s “not satisfied” with Iran’s latest proposal to end the war, citing “disjointed” leadership in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches new Soyuz-5 rocket in first test flight
video:video.video:description: A new Russian medium-class launch vehicle Soyuz-5 was launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome overnight Thursday as part of its first flight tests, Russia’s state space corporation Roscosmos said.

video:video.video:title: Returnees to southern Lebanon face difficult choices as they survey their flattened homes
video:video.video:description: Residents of southern Lebanon on Thursday surveyed damaged homes and buildings flattened to the ground.

video:video.video:title: Chedrick Greene wins special election, ensuring Democrats keep state Senate control in Michigan
video:video.video:description: Democrat Chedrick Greene won a special election in Michigan, securing his party’s control of the state Senate through the remainder of Gov.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish says it's a "gift" to see her fans in live concert film
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish says it’s a "gift" to see her fans on screen during her concert movie "Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)"

video:video.video:title: Palestinian father-to-be killed during Israeli military raid as West Bank violence continues
video:video.video:description: Nayef Firas Ziad Samaro, 26, was killed, according to the Health Ministry, and his body was brought to the hospital where his wife was giving birth.

video:video.video:title: Zac Posen's Met Gala masterpiece
video:video.video:description: Here's how designer Zac Posen dressed Kendall Jenner as a Greek goddess at the Met Gala

video:video.video:title: What to know about hantavirus, the illness suspected in a cruise ship outbreak
video:video.video:description: Three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi's husband voices family anguish over health fears
video:video.video:description: The health of imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi was at “very high risk," her foundation and family said Saturday, adding that Iran's Intelligence Ministry was opposing her transfer to Tehran for treatment by her own doctors.

video:video.video:title: Faith leaders in Ohio call on state legislators to repeal state's death penalty
video:video.video:description: A group of faith leaders representing hundreds of clergy members from across Ohio delivered a letter to state lawmakers urging them to repeal the state’s death penalty.

video:video.video:title: UN chief pays tribute to television pioneer Ted Turner, who has died at age 87
video:video.video:description: Ted Turner, a brash television pioneer who raced yachts, owned huge chunks of the American West and transformed the news business by launching CNN and introducing the 24-hour cable news cycle, died Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says 'ceasefire is not over' despite Iranian strikes
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the shaky U.S.-Iran ceasefire remains intact despite recent Iranian strikes.

video:video.video:title: South African police officer hoists crocodile from crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
video:video.video:description: A South African police captain who hoisted a crocodile out of a river in a daring effort to recover human remains has been praised for his "extraordinary bravery".

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 6th: Trump threatens bombing if Iran doesn't reopen strait; China raises Iran war diplomacy before Trump-Xi summit; TV pioneer Ted Turner, founder of CNN, dies at 87; Klimt ceiling paintings displayed in Vienna theater.

video:video.video:title: Rihanna arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Rihanna arrives at the Met Gala in custom Maison Margiela with A$AP Rocky.

video:video.video:title: Federal authorities arrest 18 for alleged drug distribution around Los Angeles park
video:video.video:description: Federal authorities said they arrested 18 people Wednesday on charges related to selling illicit drugs like fentanyl and methamphetamine around a Los Angeles park.

video:video.video:title: Romania performer defends 'Choke Me' song that has stirred Eurovision contest controversy
video:video.video:description: Romania's Eurovision singer reacted Sunday to the controversy around her team's song “Choke Me", their entry for this year’s Eurovision, as her and her team prepared to fly to Vienna for the singing contest final.

video:video.video:title: Handwritten letters in a 40-year-old pen pal relationship still goes on strong
video:video.video:description: Associated Press reporter Holly Ramer shares why she continues to handwrite letters to her New Zealand pen pal of 40 years. (AP video: Holly Ramer)

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'Very good talks' with Iran over the past 24 hours
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says the U.S. has "had very good talks" with Iran over the last 24 hours, adding "it's very possible we'll make a deal."

video:video.video:title: Cardi B arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Cardi B makes her debut at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike hits Lebanese coastal village of Saksakieh
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike that destroyed a house in the coastal village of Saksakieh in southern Lebanon killed four people and injured 33 others, the Lebanese health ministry said on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Footage said to show Ukrainian sea drone approaching Russian tanker in the Black Sea
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Sunday that Ukrainian forces had hit two Russian tankers near the entrance of the Black Sea port of Novorossiysk.

video:video.video:title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak leaves Cape Verde waters
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak departed Praia, Cape Verde, on Wednesday with nearly 150 passengers and crew on board, continuing its journey toward Spain’s Canary Islands as health authorities raced to contain the spread of the disease.

video:video.video:title: Laid off Spirit flight attendant reacts to news of airline shutting down
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines announced it is going out of business after 34 years, saying Saturday it has started winding down operations.

video:video.video:title: Modi’s party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
video:video.video:description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ruling Hindu nationalist party on Monday wrested control of the state of West Bengal, an opposition stronghold, in a key election.

video:video.video:title: How a bird protection group bought a forest rich in biodiversity
video:video.video:description: A rare pocket of woodland in northeastern Serbia has been saved from being cut down. A bird protection society raised 8,000 euros ($9,500) in a crowdfunding campaign to buy Nightingale's Forest and protect the flora and fauna that live there.

video:video.video:title: What to know about US military presence in Europe as Trump seeks drawdown
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. would go “a lot further” than a reduction of 5,000 troops announced by the Pentagon. It is exposing a new rift between NATO allies.

video:video.video:title: For foreign workers in the Middle East, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
video:video.video:description: For 15 years, Mohammad Abdullah Al Mamun worked in Saudi Arabia, sending money home to his family in one of the poorest areas of Bangladesh.

video:video.video:title: Hitting the high notes: how Switzerland keeps its yodelling tradition alive
video:video.video:description: Yodelling is alive and well in the Swiss mountains. Swiss yodelling takes two distinct forms - natural yodelling, and yodel songs.

video:video.video:title: FBI searches Virginia Senate leader's office as part of corruption probe, AP source says
video:video.video:description: The FBI searched the Virginia state Senate leader's office on Wednesday as part of a corruption investigation, a person familiar with the matter said.

video:video.video:title: All-Indigenous soccer team debuts with victory in Brazil
video:video.video:description: An all-Indigenous soccer team made its professional debut on Sunday in Rio de Janeiro, securing a victory in its first match. AP video by Mario Lobao, Diarlei Rodrigues)

video:video.video:title: 'The Great Moose Migration' inspires fans to follow the Swedish slow TV hit
video:video.video:description: Swedish slow TV hit “The Great Moose Migration” is back for its 8th season offering viewers a break from fast-paced hard news and social media.

video:video.video:title: Ships still wait in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. and Iran appear to move closer to ending war
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of merchant ships remain bottled up in the Persian Gulf as the United States and Iran appear to be moving closer to an initial agreement to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Winnie Harlow begins Met Gala planning early, but keeps her look under wraps
video:video.video:description: Winnie Harlow kept her Met Gala look under wraps, offering no details, but said she begins thinking about the event long before invitations arrive. (May 1)

video:video.video:title: AP explains Iran's Foreign Minister visit to China
video:video.video:description: China’s foreign minister on Wednesday called for a comprehensive ceasefire in the Iran war, in comments that could inject new energy into stalled efforts to end the two-month conflict between the United States and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus is on the rise in Argentina, where a stricken cruise ship began its journey
video:video.video:description: Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise.

video:video.video:title: Russian glide bomb attack on Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia kills at least 10
video:video.video:description: Russian forces struck the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia with glide bombs on Tuesday, killing at least 10 and wounding 18 others, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Person shot by law enforcement near Washington Monument, Secret Service says
video:video.video:description: The person’s condition and the circumstances surrounding the shooting around 15th Street and Independence Avenue were not immediately known.

video:video.video:title: Australian Jews tell antisemitism inquiry of surge in hate before Bondi Hanukkah massacre
video:video.video:description: All witnesses called to give evidence on Monday were Jewish Australians who recounted their experiences of hatred, some speaking under pseudonyms out of fear for their safety.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 4th: U.N. mission on a task force on opening the Strait of Hormuz has been postponed; fire breaks out at Broadway theater; authorities seek suspects in lakeside shooting in Oklahoma; fish and chip prices on the rise in the UK

video:video.video:title: Alabama legislators and residents protest special session to redraw voting districts
video:video.video:description: U.S. Rep. Terri Sewell showed solidarity with local legislators at a rally outside Alabama's State House. (AP Video by Kim Chandler)

video:video.video:title: Damson Idris walks the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Damson Idris wears leather on the 2026 Met Gala carpet.
